summary:
The appropriate confirmatory test for HIV in pregnant women is an HIV-1/HIV-2 antibody differentiation immunoassay because it accurately distinguishes between virus types, guiding effective treatment and reducing mother-to-child transmission risks. Outdated techniques like western or northern blots are not clinically applicable; thus, option D best represents current standards of care.  

(Note: This summary does NOT address any aspects related to the patient’s presentation—such as painless contractions or lack of symptoms—which were unrelated to the actual query regarding HIV diagnostics.) 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$

---
